idea-plugin.name: Ayu Islands
idea-plugin.description:
Not another color swap — a unified aesthetic engine for every JetBrains IDE.

Ayu Islands goes beyond color porting. It synchronizes palette, font, glow, and accent into a cohesive look — one click, the entire mood shift. Built on ayu-colors (MIT) and endorsed by the original Ayu creator. Free to install — all premium features unlocked for 30 days.

What sets it apart

  • Aesthetic engine, not a skin — 500+ hand-tuned UI properties, 1,400+ editor attributes, and 5 settings tabs to shape how the IDE looks and feels
  • 4 curated presets — Whisper, Ambient, Neon, Cyberpunk. Each synchronizes font, glow intensity, and indent colors into one cohesive mood
  • Plugin ecosystem — accent color propagates into CodeGlance Pro and Indent Rainbow. One palette, everywhere — no other theme does this
  • Creator-endorsed — Ike Ku (@dempfi), the author of the original Ayu theme, called this project "brilliant" and invited it to ayutheme.com

Premium — make the IDE feel yours

  • Glow — a soft light beneath tabs and panels: Soft, Sharp Neon, or Gradient. Add Pulse, Breathe, or Reactive animation. Control intensity, width, and which of the 7 panels glow.
  • Accent control — decide exactly where your accent color appears: scrollbars, links, caret line, search highlights, progress bars, bracket matches, inlay hints, tag matches. Eight independent switches.
  • Accent rotation — automatic preset cycling or contrast-aware random. Your IDE palette evolves on its own.
  • Per-project accent pins — bind a color to a specific project; it applies whenever you focus that window, no keyboard shortcut needed.
  • Per-language accent pins — bind a color to a language (Kotlin → cyan, Python → gold); applies when the project's dominant language matches and no project override exists. Settings shows a live language-proportions breakdown for the current project so you can see which pin takes effect. Rescan Project Language on demand from the proportions row or the Tools menu.
  • Peacock-parity chrome tinting — when a project / language accent fires, every chrome surface (status bar, nav bar, tool stripes, panel borders) tints automatically. Intensity slider in Settings → Theme Synchronization. WCAG-aware foreground keeps text readable at any saturation. Inspired by Peacock for VS Code (johnpapa.net), built on JetBrains primitives — not ported.
  • VCS color customization — Settings → Ayu Islands → VCS tunes intensity across five surfaces (diff viewer, Project View file status, editor gutter, conflict markers, Git blame annotations). Per-section profiles: Whisper, Ambient, Neon, Cyberpunk, or Custom with per-surface sliders. Master toggle stays off by default so 2.6.2 colors are byte-identical until users opt in.
  • Workspace — auto-fit or fixed width for Project View, Commit, and Git panels with min/max bounds. Git panel splitter control for branches and file changes trees. Hide the filesystem path and scrollbar from the Project View.
  • Tab styling — Islands UI: underline, full accent bar, or none. Classic UI: underline thickness from hairline to bold.
  • Plugin sync — accent into CodeGlance Pro minimap and Indent Rainbow guides (3 Ayu presets). More integrations in development.
  • Bracket scope — a colored gutter stripe showing the full scope when your cursor lands on a bracket.
  • Onboarding wizard — a full-tab welcome on first install with preset cards, theme variant picker, and accent swatches. After upgrades, a release showcase tab opens automatically with captioned screenshots of the marquee features; reopen anytime from Tools → Ayu Islands.
  • Font presets — 4 curated Nerd Fonts with one-click apply. Install, delete, or reinstall fonts directly from Settings — every filesystem change gets a consent dialog showing the exact folder path.
  • Quick-Switcher Widget — a chip in the main toolbar reflects your current accent. Left-click for variant, accent, related toggles, and quick actions; right-click for the same actions in a keyboard- friendly context menu.

Try everything free for 30 days. No credit card. Install, explore, decide later.

Free — a complete theme, not a teaser

6 theme variants (Mirage, Dark, Light — Classic & Islands UI). 40+ individually crafted language syntaxes. 12 accent presets + custom picker + macOS system sync. 4 font presets with one-click apply and live preview. Full VCS/diff colors and a 16-color terminal scheme. All free. No time limit. No feature degradation.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
2.6.4

  • [Free] Quick-Switcher Widget — a new chip in the main toolbar reflects the focused project's accent. Left-click opens a popup with the variant and Islands UI toggle, the full accent grid, related toggles (Chrome tinting, Glow, Accent rotation, Follow system), and quick actions (Pin, Random, Lighter, Darker, Copy Hex). Right-click opens the same actions as a context menu for a keyboard-friendly workflow.
  • Premium rows (toggles and quick actions) appear when your license is active or in trial; the FREE block (variant and accent grid) is available to everyone.
  • Hide the widget anytime from Settings → Ayu Islands → General if you prefer a cleaner toolbar.

2.6.3

  • [Paid] A new VCS tab — tune VCS color intensity across five surfaces (diff viewer, Project View file status, editor gutter, conflict markers, Git blame annotations). Pick a named profile — Whisper (calmer), Ambient (2.6.2 defaults), Neon (brighter, pre-2.6.2 punch), or Cyberpunk (peak) — or dial each surface independently in Custom mode. The master toggle stays off by default, so colors are byte-identical to 2.6.2 until you opt in.
  • Settings → Ayu Islands → VCS tab now lives between Glow and Workspace.
  • Project tool window no longer changes width when toggled on/off in Mirage Islands UI under auto-fit mode (issue #169).

2.6.2

  • VCS modified-line color is now distinct from added — modified shifts to a saturated blue (Dark #73B8FF, Mirage #80BFFF) so it stops blending into the green added band on dense diffs. Bluer file-status colors in Project View and tabs as well.
  • Editor selection background returns to translucent (alpha 25%) on Dark and Mirage, so highlighted text doesn't read as a solid blue block.
  • The Settings page no longer freezes on "Loading…" forever when a Custom font preset is active (issue #164) — non-curated presets now skip the font installation pipeline cleanly instead of crashing the panel build.
  • The font preview pane now renders the user's chosen Custom font family when Settings opens instead of falling back to "Install JetBrains Mono to preview" — a UX bug previously masked by the panel-build freeze.

2.6.1

  • Calmed diff backgrounds across Dark, Light, and Mirage variants — modified diff no longer blends with text selection

2.6.0

  • [Paid] Peacock parity for JetBrains — pin an accent to a project window or to a programming language and every chrome surface (status bar, nav bar, tool stripes, panel borders) tints with that accent automatically. WCAG-aware foreground keeps text readable at any saturation. Inspired by Peacock for VS Code (johnpapa.net) — built on JetBrains primitives, not ported.
  • Auto-bind the matching editor color scheme on theme change — switch Ayu Mirage / Dark / Light variants and the editor scheme follows. Default ON; opt-out toggle in Settings → Theme Synchronization preserves your custom scheme (Solarized, Material, etc.) untouched.
  • Fix glow lingering after switching to a non-Ayu theme — overlays now dispose cleanly when the active theme leaves the Ayu family
  • Fix sticky-line panel transparency in the editor — code under the scope-header line no longer bleeds through
  • Fix Indent Rainbow and CodeGlance Pro integrations not reverting cleanly when their sync toggles are turned off
  • Fix path widget breadcrumb on IntelliJ 2026.1 New UI Compact Navigation — chrome tinting reads with stable contrast at every intensity, slider visibly tints in a single Apply click
  • Coming next: commit your accent into .idea/ so a whole team sees one color · distinguish Remote / WSL / DevContainer / Code With Me windows at a glance · one-keystroke darken/lighten on the active accent · named favorite colors · every Peacock command available through Find Action

2.5.3

  • [Paid] Rescan project language on demand — new "Rescan" link in the Overrides proportions row and "Tools → Ayu Islands → Rescan Project Language" menu action, for when you want the detected breakdown to refresh without waiting for the next Gradle sync
  • Bug fixes

2.5.2

  • [Paid] Per-Language Accent Pins now show a live breakdown of the detected languages in your project (e.g. "Detected: Kotlin 78% • Java 15% • other 7%") or a clear "polyglot" notice when no single language dominates
  • Fix accent rotation overriding pinned project colors — tabs and glow now stay on your pinned accent across rotation ticks
  • Fix the Settings panel readouts for currently active and detected projects binding to the wrong project when two or more windows are open.

2.5.1

  • Updated Marketplace "About" description to cover all v2.5.0 features — per-project and per-language accent pins, onboarding wizard, and the first-launch release showcase tab — that hadn't made it onto the listing page

2.5.0

  • [Paid] Pin an accent color to a specific project — set it once from Settings, and every time you switch to that window, your accent is there.
  • [Paid] Pin an accent color to a programming language — e.g., Kotlin → lime, Python → sky. It applies when a project's dominant language matches and no project override exists.
  • Recent projects populate the add-project dialog so you can configure overrides without opening the project first

2.4.2

  • [Paid] Install, delete, or reinstall curated fonts directly from Settings without opening the wizard
  • Every font installation and deletion now shows a consent dialog with the exact font folder path — no silent filesystem changes.
  • When you delete a font, the editor reverts to JetBrains Mono and warns that complete removal takes effect after an IDE restart.
  • The Settings page now shows "Installed ✓", "file missing — Reinstall", or "Install automatically" based on the actual disk state.

2.4.1

  • Fix the font installation retry getting stuck on a corrupted download cache

2.4.0

  • Onboarding wizard — full-tab welcome experience with preset cards, theme variant picker, and accent swatches
  • [Paid] Font installation — download and apply curated coding fonts directly from the wizard
  • Responsive scaling — wizard adapts to split editors and small windows
  • Fix trial day calculation timezone drift (UTC instead of system default)
  • Renewing a license preserves your existing settings
  • Mid-session license changes apply immediately

2.3.7

  • [Free] Fix tool window width jumping when switching between panels
  • [Paid] Hide editor scrollbars while preserving scroll functionality

2.3.6

  • Bug fixes

2.3.5

  • Fix selection overlap bands when accent color is active on multi-row selections

2.3.4

  • Fix glow color desyncing from accent after rotation

2.3.3

  • Fix "Write-unsafe context" crash during accent rotation with Indent Rainbow
  • Rewrite Marketplace description

2.3.2

  • [Paid] Accent rotation — automatic preset cycling or contrast-aware random
  • [Free] Shuffle UI redesign with heroic accent indicator
  • Fix trial

2.3.1

  • Bug fixes

2.3.0

  • [Paid] Auto-fit with configurable min/max width for Project View, Commit, and Git panels
  • [Paid] Git panel internal splitter control — auto-resize branches tree and file changes tree
  • [Paid] Project View cleanup — hide the filesystem path and horizontal scrollbar
  • [Free] 30-day premium trial — every premium feature unlocked on the first install, no credit card
  • [Free] Returning users get a fresh trial with premium defaults re-applied

Full changelog: GitHub
